description: Seeded corpus for AUDIT-0: synthetic diffs with known injected bugs (ground truth for precision/recall) plus decoys (plausible-but-not-a-bug, for precision). One planted issue per file so the line-tolerance matcher never has ambiguous attribution. Each item carries both a raw diff (diff_path) and a prose_summary (the editorialized 'было' input variant for AUDIT-2's prose-vs-raw comparison). bug 'line' = the new-file line number of the offending line within that file.

items.prose_summary: Adds `run_probe()` in `owlex/probe.py`, which launches a subprocess via asyncio and awaits its output under a timeout before returning the decoded stdout; and `decode_stream()` in `owlex/probe_io.py`, which decodes raw bytes to a UTF-8 string.

bugs.description: The asyncio.TimeoutError branch returns without proc.kill()+await proc.wait(); the timed-out child is orphaned (the exact reap-on-timeout failure mode hardened in second_opinion._terminate).

decoys.description: decode(..., errors='replace') looks like silent error-hiding but is the intentional owlex decode pattern — behavior-safe, not a bug.

items.prose_summary: Adds two env-config readers: `load_timeout()` in `owlex/config_timeout.py` reads `OWLEX_PROBE_TIMEOUT` and returns it as an int; `strict_enabled()` in `owlex/config_flag.py` reads `OWLEX_STRICT` and returns whether strict mode is enabled.

bugs.description: os.getenv returns None when the var is unset; int(None) raises TypeError. Missing default — the documented 'pin via env with a default' pattern is violated.
Bare 'except Exception: return True' turns any failure (missing var KeyError) into strict-mode-ON, silently masking the misconfiguration instead of surfacing it.

items.prose_summary: Adds `record()` in `owlex/derivations_writer.py`, which looks up a session row by id and returns its id; and `emit_pairwise()` in `owlex/pairwise_sink.py`, which schedules a pairwise analytics write for a payload.

bugs.description: find_session can return None on a miss; row.id then raises AttributeError. No None guard.
asyncio.create_task() for a DB write inside request scope — cancelled when the request-scoped loop tears down (the documented owlex anti-pattern; must go through derivations.emit()).

items.prose_summary: Adds `last_n()` in `owlex/jsonl_tail.py`, which returns the final n items of a list; and `coerce_count()` in `owlex/jsonl_guard.py`, which normalizes a count argument to a non-negative int.

bugs.description: items[len(items) - n:] goes negative when n > len(items), wrapping the slice and returning the wrong tail; should be items[-n:] or a guarded max(0, ...).

decoys.description: n = int(n) is a redundant cast on an already-int parameter — defensive-but-pointless, behavior-neutral; not a bug.

items.prose_summary: Adds `assign()` in `owlex/anonymize_map.py`, which maps a list of names onto anonymized labels; and `store_value()` in `owlex/anonymize_sink.py`, which rounds a numeric value and stores it under a key.

bugs.description: LABELS[i] raises IndexError once len(names) exceeds len(LABELS)=6; no modulo/extension. Council seats can exceed 6.
Bare 'except Exception' silently stores a non-numeric value when round() TypeErrors, hiding bad input rather than failing closed.

items.prose_summary: Adds `elapsed_since()` in `owlex/heartbeat.py`, which computes how long since a task's last output; `is_stalled()` in `owlex/heartbeat_check.py`, which decides whether a task is stalled from an elapsed time and a limit; and `is_anonymous()` in `owlex/heartbeat_label.py`, which checks whether a name is anonymous.

bugs.description: task.get('last_output_monotonic') is None when the key is absent; 'now - last' then raises TypeError. No default/guard.
Comparison is flipped: a task is stalled when MORE than 'limit' has elapsed, so this must be 'elapsed > limit', not 'elapsed < limit'.

decoys.description: 'name == None' is a style smell (should be 'is None') but behavior-identical — not a bug.

items.prose_summary: Extends `owlex/council_config.py` with a quorum/majority policy — adds a `quorum()` helper and an `is_majority()` check — alongside the existing seat-model, reasoning, label, retry, and timeout helpers in that module.

bugs.description: quorum() returns n_seats // 2; a strict majority of n needs floor(n/2)+1, so is_majority() counts a tied half-vote (e.g. 3 of 6) as a majority. A buried off-by-one in a large file — the AUDIT-2 large-diff probe.

items.prose_summary: Reworks the quantile index computation in `owlex/rating_stats.py` and extends the rating aggregation — adds a `spread()` helper and a `p100` field to `aggregate_seat()` — in that stats module.

bugs.description: quantile() drops the `min(..., len-1)` clamp on idx; at q=1.0 (now exercised by the new p100 stat) idx == len(ordered) → IndexError. A subtle one-line guard removal buried in a large file — the AUDIT-2 subtle-large-diff probe.

items.prose_summary: Adds `retry_delays()` in `owlex/retry_budget.py`, which builds the list of backoff delays for a retry loop by calling `compute_delay()` (imported from `owlex/backoff.py`) for each attempt up to the configured maximum.

bugs.description: range(max_attempts + 1) schedules one extra retry beyond max_attempts (off-by-one); should be range(max_attempts). The genuine in-file bug — its citation resolves and survives AUDIT-1 verification.

items.bait: Calls compute_delay() imported from owlex/backoff.py — a module deliberately NOT part of this corpus item, so it is absent from the materialized post-image. The code + comments invite a reviewer to reason about the (unseen) backoff cap and cite owlex/backoff.py:<n>; that citation cannot be opened against the post-image, so AUDIT-1 verification drops it. This is the hallucination magnet that gives the verify pass something to drop — it is intentionally NOT a labeled bug/decoy.
